wikitext:
{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |Alapin Sicilian |eco=[[Chess/ECOB|B22]] |parent=[[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c5|Sicilian defence]] → [[../|Alapin variation]] }} == 2...d5 == Black is threatening to take on e4. White's best option is to take first, with [[/3. exd5|'''3. exd5''']]. After [[/3. exd5|'''3. exd5''']] the main line continues 3...Qxd5, the Barmen variation, and White doesn't have Nc3 to kick the queen away. After 4. d4, it is possible for Black to win a pawn and go into an endgame,4...cxd4 5. cxd4 Nc6 6. Nf3 Bg4 7. Nc3 Bxf3 8. gxf3 Qxd4 9. Qxd4 Nxd4 10. Nb5!{{chess/not|+}} however White has adequate compensation and better practical chances. A more common plan is to leave White with an isolated queen's pawn position and control d5 with ...e6 and ...Nf6.e.g. 4...Nc6 5. Nf3 Bg4 6. Be2 cxd4 7. cxd4 e6 8. Nc3 Qa5 9. h3 Bh5 10. O-O Nf6{{chess/not|+}}. Black's other line is 3...Nf6, christened the anti-Alapin gambit, which is similar to the [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c5/2. f4/2...d5|Tal gambit]]. White has the time to defend the d-pawn, but Black is eager to give some material for rapid development, e.g. 4. c4 e6 5. dxe6 Bxe6. === Bad moves === A quick round up of the White's other turn three options, which they should discard: White cannot play Nc3 to defend the pawn, as their c-pawn already occupies c3. '''3. e5!?''' allows Black to play ...Bf5 & ...e6, reaching an advance French-style position with the strict improvement that Black has been able to develop their queen's bishop outside of the pawn chain first. Black has equalised. '''3. d3?!''' to defend e4 leaves the pawn on c3 out of place. If White avoids taking on d5, they allow Black easy development and plenty of space, e.g. 3...Nc6 4. Nd2 Nf6 5. Ngf3 e5 6. Be2 Be7 7. O-O O-O{{chess/not|-}}. Black could also consider playing d4 themselves: 3...d4 4. cxd4 cxd4 5. Qa4+ Nc6 6. Nf3{{chess/not|-}}. '''3. d4?''' and Black is better after 3...dxe4 4. dxc5 Qxd1+ 5. Kxd1 Nf6{{chess/not|-}}. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} :'''1.e4 c5 2.c3 d5'''
3
Sicilian, Alapin [[/3. exd5|exd5]]
Qxd5
[[/3. e5|e5]]
Nc6
